mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic expansion, increasing corporate earnings prospects and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain negative in 60%+ of quarters with GDPC1 growth above 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in FEDFUNDS above 2.5% signals tightening monetary policy, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns exceed 1.5% in 50%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS rises above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate inflationary pressure with tightening, creating headwinds for equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL both rise >0.5% in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of periods with concurrent FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL increases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) signals tight labor markets, boosting corporate margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of periods with PAYEMS >150k and UNRATE <4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust manufacturing activity, increasing corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 40%+ of months with INDPRO growth above 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) signal weak demand for construction, reducing related sector revenues and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 50%+ of periods with HOUST declines >5%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% erodes purchasing power and prompts central bank intervention, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns exceed 1% in 50%+ of months with CPIAUCSL above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, boosting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 40%+ of months with RSAFS growth above 1%.
